understanding.goal_understanding: Finish the current BP35 level (and subsequent levels) by navigating the tile grid to reach the goal gem tile marked `+` without dying, using at most 2 attempts per level and 50 turns per attempt.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn you can MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT (horizontal only); after a horizontal move the character automatically continues moving in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid/support tile, hitting a hazard, or reaching the `+` goal. Gravity direction can be UP or DOWN and determines which way you fall after each move. CLICK removes breakable/clickable tiles (e.g., `x`) at a specified coordinate without moving the player and without consuming platform-timer moves (when a moving platform exists). Landing on spikes (`u`/`v`) immediately ends the attempt. UNDO reverts the previous action to recover from a bad move/click. The structured state’s landing previews (left_landing/right_landing and corridor_scan) indicate where you will end up after gravity resolves, helping choose safe moves and prioritize passages that change y-level.

understanding.goal_understanding: Finish the current BP35 level (and subsequent levels) by navigating the tile grid to reach the goal gem tile marked `+` without dying, using at most 2 attempts per level and 50 turns per attempt.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn you can MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT (horizontal only); after a horizontal move the character automatically continues moving in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid/support tile, hitting a hazard, or reaching the `+` goal. Gravity direction can be UP or DOWN and determines which way you fall after each move. CLICK removes breakable/clickable tiles (e.g., `x`) at a specified coordinate without moving the player and without consuming platform-timer moves (when a moving platform exists). Landing on spikes (`u`/`v`) immediately ends the attempt. UNDO reverts the previous action to recover from a bad move/click. The structured state’s landing previews (left_landing/right_landing and corridor_scan) indicate where you will end up after gravity resolves, helping choose safe moves and prioritize passages that change y-level.
